name: Seafood Rice

ingredients.item: extra-virgin olive oil, plus 1 teaspoon, divided
seafood (squid, cut into rounds, large shrimp, and mussels)
onion, chopped
sea salt
garlic cloves, minced
chopped celery
medium tomatoes, chopped, or 8 ounces cherry tomatoes
dry white wine
arborio rice
chopped fresh parsley
chopped fresh dill
chicken, vegetable, or fish broth, divided
Lemon wedges, for serving
In a large skillet over medium-high heat, heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil.
Add the squid and cook for about 2 minutes. Remove the squid and set aside.
Add the remaining 1 teaspoon of olive oil to the skillet to heat.
Add the onion and salt. Cook for 5 minutes, until translucent.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute more.
Add the celery and tomatoes. Cook for 3 minutes.
Pour in the wine and cook for about 3 minutes, stirring frequently.

Stir in the rice, parsley, dill, and 4 cups of broth. Cover the skillet and simmer for 15 minutes. After 15 minutes, most of the liquid should be absorbed, but there should still be a little left. If there isn’t, add the remaining broth.
Top the rice mixture with the shrimp and mussels, cover the skillet, and simmer for 5 minutes more, until the shrimp are just cooked.
Return the squid to the skillet. Discard any unopened mussels. Serve with the lemon wedges on the side.
Cover and refrigerate any leftovers for up to 3 days.
